questions.prompt: A customer has a 'professional certification' string that the standard Microsoft 365 schema doesn't carry. Where do you store it so signatures can render it without a per-user template?
choices: Hardcode it into a variant of the signature template, one per user
Use one of the Microsoft 365 Additional Attributes (extension attributes) and reference it as a field in the template
Put it into the Brand Kit's disclaimer text
Add it as a Senders rule on each user
questions.explanation: Additional Attributes (or Google Custom Attributes for Workspace) extend the directory schema for off-schema data. The Fields elements expose them like standard fields, so one template renders correctly per user.
